_source: Environment (Protection) Rules, 1986 — Schedule VI, General Standards for Discharge of Environmental Pollutants (CPCB). Verified 2026-07 against cpcb.nic.in/generalstandards.pdf and independent mirrors. Factory CTO / SPCB limits OVERRIDE these and are often stricter.

_notes.bod: BOD basis is 3 days at 27 C (Indian standard), NOT 5 days at 20 C.
_notes.temperature: The general standard is a DELTA: 'shall not exceed 5 C above the receiving-water temperature' — not an absolute cap. The numeric max below is a pragmatic absolute PROXY used for screening only; set the real absolute limit from the factory CTO.
_notes.tds: TDS is NOT a CPCB general standard. It is sector/CTO-specific (e.g. distillery, pharma). The value below is a CTO placeholder default, clearly flagged, not a general limit.
_notes.fecal_coliform: Not part of the general effluent standards (comes from IS:2296 / STP norms). Deliberately omitted here.

tds._note: NOT a CPCB general standard — set from factory CTO
NOT a CPCB general standard — set from factory CTO
